response.justification: The candidate is a strong web and full-stack engineer but fundamentally misaligned with the core requirement. C++ appears only as a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; it is not even listed in the skills section. All recent and current work is TypeScript, Node.js, and web-stack. There is no evidence of STL usage, multi-threaded C++ systems, message-broker or MQTT experience, industrial automation context, SDL practices, or familiarity with IEC 62443 or any industrial protocol. The role demands a senior C++ engineer with an industrial systems background, which this resume does not reflect.
strengths: Extensive event-driven and distributed systems experience (GISCollective platform, Sociomantic high-throughput microservices)
Consistent application of agile engineering practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — across multiple employers
Broad REST API and multi-language background demonstrating adaptability across stacks
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old (6 months at National Instruments, 2014–2015); absent from the skills section entirely
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA) exposure
No evidence of multi-threaded, high-performance, or embedded C++ systems development beyond a bachelor thesis side project
